content:
# Joshua Chapter 3 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 227-228 | Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 164-165 (LXX)
**Source Images:** `JOS_003_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_003_page_002.jpg`; `JOS_003_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_003_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

### Israel Prepares to Cross the Jordan (vv. 1-6)

**1** And Joshua rose early in the morning, and they set out from Shittim and came to the Jordan, he and all the Sons of Israel, and they lodged there before they crossed over.

**2** And it came to pass at the end of three days that the officers passed through the midst of the camp,

**3** and they commanded the people, saying, "When you see the ark of the covenant of YHWH your God, and the Levitical priests carrying it, then you shall set out from your place and go after it.

**4** Yet there shall be a distance between you and it, about two thousand cubits by measure. You shall not draw near to it, so that you may know the way by which you shall go, for you have not passed this way before."

**5** And Joshua said to the people, "Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow YHWH will do wonders in your midst."

**6** And Joshua spoke to the priests, saying, "Take up the ark of the covenant and cross over before the people." And they took up the ark of the covenant and went before the people.

---

### YHWH Exalts Joshua (vv. 7-8)

**7** And YHWH said to Joshua, "This day I will begin to make you great in the eyes of all Israel, so that they may know that, as I was with Moses, so I will be with you.

**8** And you shall command the priests who carry the ark of the covenant, saying, 'When you come to the edge of the waters of the Jordan, you shall stand still in the Jordan.'"

---

### Joshua Announces the Living God (vv. 9-13)

**9** And Joshua said to the Sons of Israel, "Draw near here and hear the words of YHWH your God."

**10** And Joshua said, "By this you shall know that the living God is in your midst, and that he will surely drive out from before you the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Hivite, and the Perizzite, and the Girgashite, and the Amorite, and the Jebusite.

**11** Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth is crossing over before you into the Jordan.

**12** And now, take for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Israel, one man for each tribe.

**13** And it shall come to pass, when the soles of the feet of the priests who carry the ark of YHWH, the Lord of all the earth, rest in the waters of the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan shall be cut off — the waters that come down from above — and they shall stand in one heap."

---

### The Waters Are Cut Off (vv. 14-17)

**14** And it came to pass, when the people set out from their tents to cross over the Jordan, with the priests carrying the ark of the covenant before the people,

**15** and when those carrying the ark came to the Jordan, and the feet of the priests carrying the ark were dipped in the edge of the waters — now the Jordan overflows all its banks throughout the days of harvest —

**16** the waters coming down from above stood still; they rose up in one heap, very far away, at Adam, the city that is beside Zarethan; and those flowing down toward the sea of the Arabah, the Salt Sea, were completely cut off. And the people crossed over opposite Jericho.

**17** And the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan, while all Israel was crossing over on dry ground, until all the nation had finished crossing over the Jordan.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Transliteration & Divine Name
- **YHWH** (יהוה): Retained as the tetragrammaton throughout, per LOGOS convention. Not rendered "the LORD."
- **Lord of all the earth** (v. 11, 13): Hebrew אֲדוֹן כָּל־הָאָרֶץ (*Adon kol-ha'aretz*). Here אָדוֹן is the common noun "lord/master," not the divine name, so it is rendered "Lord" (capitalized as a title) rather than YHWH.

### Key Lexical Decisions
- **Sons of Israel** (בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל): Retained literally per LOGOS policy; not "children of Israel" or "Israelites."
- **Shittim** (הַשִּׁטִּים, v. 1): Transliterated. The name means "the acacias"; the LXX (Vaticanus) reads Σαττιν. This was Israel's final encampment east of the Jordan (cf. Num 25:1).
- **Consecrate yourselves** (הִתְקַדְּשׁוּ, v. 5): Hitpael of קדשׁ, reflexive — "make yourselves holy/set apart." Ritual preparation before a theophanic event.
- **Wonders** (נִפְלָאוֹת, v. 5): Niphal participle of פלא, denoting extraordinary divine acts. The LXX renders θαυμαστά.
- **Stand in one heap** (נֵד אֶחָד, vv. 13, 16): Hebrew נֵד means a "heap" or "mound" of water. The same term appears in Exodus 15:8 and Psalm 78:13, linking the Jordan crossing to the Red Sea event.

### Textual Notes
- **v. 4 — "about two thousand cubits"**: The MT specifies כְּאַלְפַּיִם אַמָּה, approximately 3,000 feet (900 meters). The LXX agrees (δισχιλίους πήχεις).
- **v. 10 — Seven nations**: The MT lists seven Canaanite peoples. The order varies slightly across manuscripts; the Leningrad Codex reads: Canaanite, Hittite, Hivite, Perizzite, Girgashite, Amorite, Jebusite. The LXX (Vaticanus) has a slightly different order but the same seven nations.
- **v. 15 — "the Jordan overflows all its banks"**: A parenthetical note in the MT (וְהַיַּרְדֵּן מָלֵא עַל־כָּל־גְּדוֹתָיו כֹּל יְמֵי קָצִיר) explaining that the crossing occurred during the spring harvest flood, making the miracle more dramatic. The barley harvest in the Jordan Valley falls in March-April.
- **v. 16 — Adam / Zarethan**: Adam (אָדָם) is identified with modern Tell ed-Damiyeh, approximately 18 miles north of Jericho. Zarethan (צָרְתָן) is nearby. The waters were dammed far upstream, allowing the riverbed to dry at the crossing point.
- **v. 11 — "the Lord of all the earth"**: The title אֲדוֹן כָּל־הָאָרֶץ emphasizes YHWH's universal sovereignty — not merely a tribal deity but master of all creation. This is theologically significant as Israel enters Canaan, the land of other "gods."

### Structural Note
Joshua 3 forms a literary parallel with Exodus 14 (the Red Sea crossing). Both feature: (a) divine command, (b) priestly/Levitical involvement, (c) waters parting, (d) the people crossing on dry ground. The deliberate echo confirms Joshua as Moses' true successor (v. 7) and YHWH as the same God who delivered Israel from Egypt.
